Gumroad MCP server model context protocol

gumroad.com/api sales subscribers offer codes resource subscriptions endpoints

researched_category.notes: Gumroad is a platform for creators to sell digital products, memberships, and physical goods directly to customers.

one_liner.value: Digital commerce platform for creators to sell products, memberships, and digital goods directly to their audience.

methods.notes: The API page describes OAuth 2.0 application registration but also provides 'Generate access token' for personal use. n8n docs confirm both methods.

access.notes: Gumroad pricing page shows no monthly fee (10% + $0.50 per transaction). Any free account can create an API application under Settings > Advanced. No paid plan or admin approval required.

summary.value: Gumroad v2 REST API at api.gumroad.com/v2 provides endpoints for Products (list/get/enable/disable), Sales (list/filter), Subscribers (list/get), Offer Codes (CRUD), License Keys (verify/enable/disable), Resource Subscriptions (webhooks), and User profile. Notably does NOT support creating products via API, and there is no official test/sandbox environment. The API page also documents a CLI wrapper. Some community references mention a conceptual GraphQL schema but official docs are REST-only.

agent_interface.notes: A community MCP server (rmarescu/gumroad-mcp) exists with 22+ stars, providing tools for products, sales, offer codes, and user data. Composio also has a Gumroad toolkit with 7 tools and OAuth2 support. Gumroad also provides an official CLI tool.

rationale.value: Gumroad has a well-documented public REST API, offers self-serve free API access (no paid plan or admin approval needed), and already has a community MCP server (rmarescu/gumroad-mcp) and Composio toolkit available. The API covers the main use cases an agent would need: listing products, querying sales, managing offer codes, verifying licenses, and subscribing to webhook events. The only notable gap is the lack of a 'create product' endpoint, which limits provisioning workflows but doesn't block most agentic use cases (analysis, monitoring, customer management).

s1.quote: The Gumroad OAuth API is based around REST. We return JSON for every request... On the application page, click Generate access token to get the token you will use with the API.
s1.title: Gumroad API Documentation

s2.quote: Gumroad doesn't charge you a monthly fee. Instead, our fees are deducted as a small percentage of every sale.
s2.title: Gumroad Pricing

s3.quote: Supported authentication methods: API access token, OAuth2
s3.title: Gumroad credentials - n8n Docs

s4.quote: Gumroad is a platform for selling digital products, physical goods, and memberships... Tools: 7... Managed auth: Managed by Composio
s4.title: Gumroad Toolkit - Composio

s5.quote: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server implementation for Gumroad API
s5.title: rmarescu/gumroad-mcp on GitHub

s6.quote: I have been searching for a while and read the API documentation... not able to find any API endpoint which could allow me to create a product
s6.title: StackOverflow: How can one create a product through Gumroad API

s7.quote: API Features: View information about your products, Add, edit, and delete offer codes, variants, and custom fields... Subscribe to sales notifications, Verify licenses, Retrieve subscriber details
s7.title: Gumroad API Essential Guide - Rollout

s8.quote: Gumroad MCP Server is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server implementation designed for Gumroad's API, allowing MCP-compatible AI clients to interact seamlessly with Gumroad's services.
s8.title: Gumroad MCP Server on MCP.so

s9.quote: Your advanced settings allow you to: ... Create an application to tap into Gumroad's API.
s9.title: Account settings - Gumroad Help Center

limitations: The API does not support creating products (no POST /products endpoint)
No official sandbox/test environment for development
API docs are somewhat lacking in detail (no OpenAPI/Swagger spec)
The API is primarily read-focused; write operations are limited to offer codes, variants, and custom fields
